content.question:
Finish each of the following sentences in such a way that it means exactly the same as the sentence printed before it.

1. As he gets richer, he spends less money.
👉 The richer ___
content.answer: he gets, the less money he spends. || he gets, the less money he spends / The richer he gets, the less money he spends. / The richer he gets, the less money he spends

content.question:
Finish each of the following sentences in such a way that it means exactly the same as the sentence printed before it.

2. Jane didn't study, and this led to her failure.
👉 Jane's failure ___
content.answer: was caused by her lack of study. / was due to her lack of study. || was caused by her lack of study / was due to her lack of study / Jane's failure was caused by her lack of study. / Jane's failure was caused by her lack of study / Jane's failure was due to her lack of study. / Jane's failure was due to her lack of study

content.question:
Finish each of the following sentences in such a way that it means exactly the same as the sentence printed before it.

3. Whales are rarely seen at this time of year.
👉 There are ___
content.answer: few whales seen at this time of year. / few sightings of whales at this time of year. || few whales seen at this time of year / few sightings of whales at this time of year / There are few whales seen at this time of year. / There are few whales seen at this time of year / There are few sightings of whales at this time of year. / There are few sightings of whales at this time of year

content.question:
Finish each of the following sentences in such a way that it means exactly the same as the sentence printed before it.

4. It is open to question whether the candidate will succeed.
👉 It is not ___
content.answer: certain that the candidate will succeed. || certain that the candidate will succeed / It is not certain that the candidate will succeed. / It is not certain that the candidate will succeed

content.question:
Finish each of the following sentences in such a way that it means exactly the same as the sentence printed before it.

5. Everyone left as soon as she started singing.
👉 No sooner ___
content.answer: did she start singing than everyone left. / had she started singing than everyone left. || did she start singing than everyone left / had she started singing than everyone left / No sooner did she start singing than everyone left. / No sooner did she start singing than everyone left / No sooner had she started singing than everyone left. / No sooner had she started singing than everyone left

content.question:
Finish each of the following sentences in such a way that it means exactly the same as the sentence printed before it.

6. A car like that will cost us at least £20,000.
👉 We won't be able ___
content.answer: to buy a car like that for less than £20,000. || to buy a car like that for less than £20,000 / We won't be able to buy a car like that for less than £20,000. / We won't be able to buy a car like that for less than £20,000 / We will not be able to buy a car like that for less than £20,000. / We will not be able to buy a car like that for less than £20,000

content.question:
Finish each of the following sentences in such a way that it means exactly the same as the sentence printed before it.

7. My story has been rejected.
👉 I have received ___
content.answer: a rejection for my story. || a rejection for my story / I have received a rejection for my story. / I have received a rejection for my story / I've received a rejection for my story. / I've received a rejection for my story

content.question:
Finish each of the following sentences in such a way that it means exactly the same as the sentence printed before it.

8. My teacher assured me that I would pass the exam.
👉 According ___
content.answer: to my teacher, I am sure to pass the exam. / to my teacher, I was sure to pass the exam. || to my teacher, I am sure to pass the exam / to my teacher, I was sure to pass the exam / According to my teacher, I am sure to pass the exam. / According to my teacher, I am sure to pass the exam / According to my teacher, I was sure to pass the exam. / According to my teacher, I was sure to pass the exam / According to my teacher I am sure to pass the exam / According to my teacher I was sure to pass the exam

content.question:
For each of the sentences below write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence, but using the word given. This word must not be altered in any way.

9. I'm sure I was right to turn down the job.
content.answer: I have no regrets about turning down the job. || I have no regrets about turning down the job / I've no regrets about turning down the job. / I've no regrets about turning down the job

content.question:
For each of the sentences below write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence, but using the word given. This word must not be altered in any way.

10. You'll probably hear from them before Friday.
content.answer: You're likely to hear from them before Friday. / It's likely you'll hear from them before Friday. || You're likely to hear from them before Friday / You are likely to hear from them before Friday. / You are likely to hear from them before Friday / It's likely you'll hear from them before Friday / It is likely you will hear from them before Friday. / It is likely you will hear from them before Friday / It's likely you will hear from them before Friday. / It's likely you will hear from them before Friday / It is likely you'll hear from them before Friday. / It is likely you'll hear from them before Friday

content.question:
For each of the sentences below write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence, but using the word given. This word must not be altered in any way.

11. Joe thinks he is always right.
content.answer: Joe thinks he never makes mistakes. || Joe thinks he never makes mistakes / Joe thinks that he never makes mistakes. / Joe thinks that he never makes mistakes

content.question:
For each of the sentences below write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence, but using the word given. This word must not be altered in any way.

12. We have decided to go to Crete for the summer.
content.answer: Our intention is to go to Crete for the summer. || Our intention is to go to Crete for the summer / It is our intention to go to Crete for the summer. / It is our intention to go to Crete for the summer / It's our intention to go to Crete for the summer. / It's our intention to go to Crete for the summer

content.question:
For each of the sentences below write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence, but using the word given. This word must not be altered in any way.

13. The meeting was held in the Town Hall.
content.answer: The meeting took place in the Town Hall. || The meeting took place in the Town Hall

content.question:
For each of the sentences below write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence, but using the word given. This word must not be altered in any way.

14. It was stupid of you to give up English.
content.answer: You were an idiot to give up English. || You were an idiot to give up English / You were such an idiot to give up English. / You were such an idiot to give up English

content.question:
For each of the sentences below write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence, but using the word given. This word must not be altered in any way.

15. They managed to buy the house only because we lent them the money.
content.answer: Without our loan they wouldn't have managed to buy the house. || Without our loan they wouldn't have managed to buy the house / Without our loan, they wouldn't have managed to buy the house. / Without our loan, they wouldn't have managed to buy the house / Without our loan they would not have managed to buy the house. / Without our loan they would not have managed to buy the house / Without our loan, they would not have managed to buy the house. / Without our loan, they would not have managed to buy the house / They wouldn't have managed to buy the house without our loan. / They wouldn't have managed to buy the house without our loan / They would not have managed to buy the house without our loan. / They would not have managed to buy the house without our loan

content.question:
For each of the sentences below write a new sentence as similar as possible in meaning to the original sentence, but using the word given. This word must not be altered in any way.

16. The only reason I came was to see you.
content.answer: My sole reason for coming was to see you. || My sole reason for coming was to see you / The sole reason I came was to see you. / The sole reason I came was to see you / My sole reason to come was to see you. / My sole reason to come was to see you
